statement: Intel detailed its Crescent Island data-center inference GPU at Computex 2026, using LPDDR5X (up to 480GB) instead of HBM to avoid advanced-packaging and HBM contention, with a second-half 2026 sampling target.
why: A memory choice that sidesteps HBM and CoWoS could add alternative merchant-GPU supply, but Intel is still pre-sampling with the less-adopted oneAPI stack, so near-term traction is minimal.

impact.mechanism: An LPDDR5X inference accelerator adds a non-HBM alternative supply path that does not draw on scarce HBM or advanced packaging.

evidence.source: Tom's Hardware Intel Crescent Island Computex 2026
evidence.excerpt: It forgoes GDDR or HBM memory for LPDDR5X

confidence.basis: Secondary trade press, single source; product is pre-sampling with no shipping revenue.
